# services.sharkey

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.sharkey`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.sharkey.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Sharkey, a Sharkish microblogging platform. |
| `services.sharkey.environmentFiles` | `list of absolute path` | List of paths to files containing environment variables for Sharkey to use at runtime. This is useful for keeping secrets out of the Nix store. See <https://docs.joinsharkey.org/docs/install/configuration/> for how to configure Sharkey using environment variables. |
| `services.sharkey.openFirewall` | `boolean` | Whether to open ports in the NixOS firewall for Sharkey. |
| `services.sharkey.package` | `package` | The sharkey package to use. |
| `services.sharkey.settings` | `open submodule of (YAML 1.1 value)` | Configuration options for Sharkey. See <https://activitypub.software/TransFem-org/Sharkey/-/blob/develop/.config/example.yml> for a list of all available configuration options. |
| `services.sharkey.settings.address` | `string` | The address that Sharkey binds to. |
| `services.sharkey.settings.fulltextSearch.provider` | `one of "sqlLike", "sqlPgroonga", "sqlTsvector", "meilisearch"` | Which provider to use for full text search. All options other than `sqlLike` require extra setup - see the comments in <https://activitypub.software/TransFem-org/Sharkey/-/blob/develop/.config/example.yml> for details. If `sqlPgroonga` is set, and `services.sharkey.setupPostgres` is `true`, the pgroonga extension will automatically be setup. You still need to create an index manually. If using Meilisearch, consider setting `services.sharkey.setupMeilisearch` instead, which will configure Meilisearch for you. |
| `services.sharkey.settings.id` | `one of "aid", "aidx", "meid", "ulid", "objectid"` | The ID generation method for Sharkey to use. Do NOT change this after initial setup! |
| `services.sharkey.settings.mediaDirectory` | `absolute path` | Path to the folder where Sharkey stores uploaded media such as images and attachments. |
| `services.sharkey.settings.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | The port that Sharkey will listen on. |
| `services.sharkey.settings.socket` | `null or absolute path` | If specified, creates a UNIX socket at the given path that Sharkey listens on. |
| `services.sharkey.settings.url` | `string` | The full URL that the Sharkey instance will be publically accessible on. Do NOT change this after initial setup! |
| `services.sharkey.setupMeilisearch` | `boolean` | Whether to automatically set up a local Meilisearch instance and configure Sharkey to use it. You need to ensure `services.meilisearch.masterKeyFile` is correctly configured for a working Meilisearch setup. You also need to configure Sharkey to use an API key obtained from Meilisearch with the `MK_CONFIG_MEILISEARCH_APIKEY` environment variable, and set `services.sharkey.settings.meilisearch.index` to the created index. See <https://docs.joinsharkey.org/docs/customisation/search/meilisearch/> for how to create an API key and index. |
| `services.sharkey.setupPostgresql` | `boolean` | Whether to automatically set up a local PostgreSQL database and configure Sharkey to use it. |
| `services.sharkey.setupRedis` | `boolean` | Whether to automatically set up a local Redis cache and configure Sharkey to use it. |
